name: feat-messaging-test-harness-core
title: Add messaging test harness for application developers
description: Add Headless.Messaging.Testing — a single NuGet package with ITestHarness, observable collections (Published/Consumed/Faulted), awaitable assertion primitives, and TestConsumer<T> for testing messaging flows against an in-memory pipeline without Docker.

userStories.title: Project scaffolding
userStories.description:
TDD order: write failing test → implement minimal code → verify PASS

Create the Headless.Messaging.Testing project with correct structure, dependencies, and package metadata. Follow Headless.Testing pattern: IsTestProject=false, IsTestableProject=false, net10.0. Add project references to Headless.Messaging.Core, Headless.Messaging.InMemoryQueue, Headless.Messaging.InMemoryStorage.
acceptanceCriteria: src/Headless.Messaging.Testing/Headless.Messaging.Testing.csproj exists with correct TargetFramework, IsTestProject, and project references
dotnet build src/Headless.Messaging.Testing/ succeeds
Project added to Headless.sln

userStories.title: RecordedMessage types and MessageObservationStore
userStories.description:
TDD order: write failing test → implement minimal code → verify PASS

Define RecordedMessage record type (MessageType, Message, MessageId, CorrelationId, Headers, Topic, Timestamp, Exception) and MessageObservationStore with three ConcurrentQueue<RecordedMessage> collections (Published, Consumed, Faulted) plus TaskCompletionSource waiter lists for WaitFor* methods. On timeout throw MessageObservationTimeoutException with diagnostic payload.
acceptanceCriteria: RecordedMessage sealed record contains all required metadata fields including Exception
MessageObservationStore.Record() adds to correct collection and completes matching waiters
WaitForAsync completes immediately when matching message already exists in queue
WaitForAsync with predicate only matches when predicate returns true
WaitForAsync times out and throws MessageObservationTimeoutException with expected-vs-actual diagnostic info
Thread-safe under concurrent Record + WaitFor

userStories.title: Recording infrastructure (transport + pipeline decorators)
userStories.description:
TDD order: write failing test → implement minimal code → verify PASS

Create RecordingTransport (decorates ITransport) that records TransportMessage to Published then forwards, and RecordingConsumeExecutionPipeline (decorates IConsumeExecutionPipeline) that records Consumed on success or Faulted with exception on failure. Both reference shared MessageObservationStore.
acceptanceCriteria: RecordingTransport records message headers and deserialized body then forwards to inner transport
RecordingConsumeExecutionPipeline records Consumed on successful execution
RecordingConsumeExecutionPipeline records Faulted with exception on handler failure and re-throws
Both decorators correctly populate MessageType for typed WaitFor queries

userStories.title: MessagingTestHarness core class
userStories.description:
TDD order: write failing test → implement minimal code → verify PASS

Create MessagingTestHarness : IAsyncDisposable with static CreateAsync(Action<IServiceCollection>) that builds isolated DI container, forces in-memory transport+storage, registers recording decorators, sets single-threaded Dispatcher defaults, bootstraps pipeline. Expose Published/Consumed/Faulted collections, WaitFor* methods, GetTestConsumer<T>(), Publisher, ServiceProvider.
acceptanceCriteria: CreateAsync builds isolated DI container with in-memory transport and storage
CreateAsync bootstraps pipeline successfully without HostedService infrastructure
Published/Consumed/Faulted collections are initially empty and populate correctly after pub-sub
Two concurrent harness instances do not share state
DisposeAsync stops background tasks and cleans up without exceptions
User-configured consumers are registered and discoverable

userStories.title: TestConsumer<T> generic fixture
userStories.description:
TDD order: write failing test → implement minimal code → verify PASS

Create TestConsumer<TMessage> : IConsume<TMessage> that captures ConsumeContext into thread-safe list. Expose ReceivedContexts, ReceivedMessages (projected), Clear(). Add convenience extension AddTestConsumer<TMessage> for harness registration.
acceptanceCriteria: TestConsumer captures ConsumeContext with full metadata (MessageId, CorrelationId, Headers, Topic)
ReceivedMessages projects payloads correctly from ConsumeContext
Clear() resets all captured data (thread-safe)
GetTestConsumer<T>() on harness returns the registered singleton instance

userStories.title: End-to-end integration test suite
userStories.description:
TDD order: write failing test → implement minimal code → verify PASS

Create comprehensive test suite exercising the harness as a downstream developer would. Cover publish-consume-assert flow, fault observation, predicate filtering, timeout diagnostics, parallel isolation, DI integration with mocks, custom headers, TestConsumer workflow, and <100ms performance.
acceptanceCriteria: Publish → consume → assert test works end-to-end with ConsumeContext metadata verification
Faulted observation captures exception type and message when consumer throws
WaitForConsumed with predicate correctly filters on message properties
Timeout throws MessageObservationTimeoutException with diagnostic info showing published messages
Two concurrent harness instances in parallel do not cross-contaminate messages
Consumer with injected mock dependency resolves and processes correctly

userStories.title: Package README and XML documentation
userStories.description:
Sequential: write README → add XML docs → verify build

Create package README with quick-start example following existing package README patterns. Add XML docs to all public types and methods: MessagingTestHarness, TestConsumer<T>, RecordedMessage, MessageObservationTimeoutException.
acceptanceCriteria: README.md exists with quick-start example showing create → publish → assert pattern
All public types and methods have XML documentation comments
dotnet build with documentation warnings enabled passes

findings: All messaging services (ConsumerRegistry, Dispatcher, MemoryQueue) use instance fields, no static state — fresh ServiceCollection per test gives full isolation
ConsumerRegistry freeze-on-first-read pattern is instance-scoped in src/Headless.Messaging.Core/ConsumerRegistry.cs
Existing Headless.Testing package at src/Headless.Testing/ provides pattern: IsTestProject=false, ships as NuGet, references xunit + AwesomeAssertions
IConsumeFilter is single-slot scoped (not a chain) — recording should use pipeline decorator instead to preserve filter slot for users
ConsumeExecutionPipeline creates DI scope per message in src/Headless.Messaging.Core/Internal/IConsumeExecutionPipeline.cs
Dispatcher requires IDataStorage — InMemoryStorage package provides in-memory implementation via UseInMemoryStorage()
Bootstrapper registered as HostedService — harness needs to call StartAsync directly
TestSubscriber in tests/Headless.Messaging.Core.Tests.Harness/Helpers/TestSubscriber.cs has WaitForMessageAsync + ConsumeContext capture pattern
